content:
# Mark Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), folios 34v–35r (PDF pages 68–69) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_006_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-NT/MRK_006_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Witness:** Codex Vaticanus (B, 03) — consulted via [DigiVatLib](https://digi.vatlib.it/view/MSS_Vat.gr.1209)

---

¹ And he-went-out from-there and comes into his hometown, and his disciples follow him.

² And the Sabbath having-come, he-began to-teach in the synagogue, and the many hearing were-being-astonished, saying, "From-where [does] this-[one] [have] these-[things]? And what [is] the wisdom having-been-given to-this-[one], and such mighty-works through his hands come-into-being?

³ Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary and brother of James and Joses and Judas and Simon? And are not his sisters here with us?" And they-were-being-scandalized at him.

⁴ And Jesus was-saying to-them, "A-prophet is not without-honor except in his hometown and among his relatives and in his house."

⁵ And he-was not able there to-do any mighty-work, except upon a-few sick-[ones], having-laid the hands, he-healed.

⁶ And he-was-marveling on-account-of their unbelief. And he-was-going-around the villages in-a-circle, teaching.

⁷ And he-calls-to-[himself] the twelve and began to-send them out two-by-two, and was-giving them authority over the unclean spirits.

⁸ And he-ordered them that they-should-take nothing for [the] way except a-staff only — no bread, no bag, no copper in the belt —

⁹ but having-been-shod [with] sandals, and "do not put-on two tunics."

¹⁰ And he-was-saying to-them, "Wherever you-enter into a-house, remain there until you-go-out from-there.

¹¹ And whatever place does not receive you nor hear you, going-out from-there, shake-off the dust under your feet as a-testimony to-them."

¹² And having-gone-out, they-proclaimed that [people] should-repent,

¹³ and they-were-casting-out many demons and were-anointing with-oil many sick-[ones] and were-healing.

¹⁴ And King Herod heard, for his name became well-known, and they-were-saying, "John the-[one]-baptizing has-been-raised from [the] dead, and on-account-of-this the powers work in him."

¹⁵ But others were-saying, "He-is Elijah." And others were-saying, "A-prophet, like one of-the prophets."

¹⁶ But having-heard, Herod was-saying, "John whom I beheaded, this-one was-raised."

¹⁷ For Herod himself, having-sent, seized John and bound him in prison on-account-of Herodias, the wife of Philip his brother, because he-married her.

¹⁸ For John was-saying to Herod, "It-is not lawful for-you to-have the wife of-your brother."

¹⁹ And Herodias held-a-grudge-against him and was-wanting to-kill him, and was not able;

²⁰ for Herod was-fearing John, knowing him [to be] a-righteous and holy man, and was-protecting him. And having-heard him, he-was greatly perplexed, and gladly he-was-hearing him.

²¹ And an-opportune day having-come, when Herod on his birthday made a-banquet for his nobles and the commanders and the leading-[men] of Galilee,

²² and his daughter Herodias having-entered and having-danced, she-pleased Herod and the-ones-reclining-together. The king said to-the girl, "Ask me whatever you-want, and I-will-give [it] to-you."

²³ And he-swore to-her, "Whatever you-ask me, I-will-give to-you, up-to half of-my kingdom."

²⁴ And having-gone-out, she-said to-her mother, "What should-I-ask?" And she said, "The head of John the-[one]-baptizing."

²⁵ And having-entered immediately with haste to the king, she-asked, saying, "I-want that at-once you-give me upon a-platter the head of John the Baptist."

²⁶ And the king, having-become deeply-grieved, on-account-of the oaths and the-ones-reclining-together did not want to-refuse her.

²⁷ And immediately the king, having-sent an-executioner, ordered to-bring his head. And having-gone, he-beheaded him in the prison,

²⁸ and brought his head upon a-platter and gave it to-the girl, and the girl gave it to-her mother.

²⁹ And having-heard, his disciples came and took his body and placed it in a-tomb.

³⁰ And the apostles gather-together to Jesus and reported to-him all-[things], both what they-did and what they-taught.

³¹ And he-says to-them, "Come yourselves privately to a-deserted place and rest a-little." For the-ones coming and the-ones going were many, and they-had no-opportunity even to-eat.

³² And they-went-away in the boat to a-deserted place privately.

³³ And they-saw them going, and many recognized [them], and on-foot from all the cities they-ran-together there and arrived-before them.

³⁴ And having-come-out, he-saw a-great crowd and was-moved-with-compassion upon them, because they-were as sheep not having a-shepherd, and he-began to-teach them many-[things].

³⁵ And already [the] hour having-become late, his disciples having-come-to him were-saying, "The place is deserted and already [the] hour [is] late.

³⁶ Dismiss them, in-order-that having-gone into the surrounding countryside and villages, they-may-buy for-themselves something to-eat."

³⁷ But answering, he-said to-them, "You give them [something] to-eat." And they-say to-him, "Having-gone, shall-we-buy two-hundred denarii of-bread and give [it] to-them to-eat?"

³⁸ And he-says to-them, "How-many loaves do-you-have? Go, see." And having-known, they-say, "Five, and two fish."

³⁹ And he-ordered them to-recline all, group-by-group, upon the green grass.

⁴⁰ And they-reclined, section-by-section, by hundreds and by fifties.

⁴¹ And having-taken the five loaves and the two fish, having-looked-up to heaven, he-blessed and broke the loaves and was-giving to the disciples in-order-that they-might-set-before them, and the two fish he-divided to-all.

⁴² And all ate and were-satisfied.

⁴³ And they-took-up twelve baskets full of-fragments and from the fish.

⁴⁴ And the-ones having-eaten the loaves were five-thousand men.

⁴⁵ And immediately he-compelled his disciples to-embark into the boat and to-go-before to the other-side, to Bethsaida, while he himself dismisses the crowd.

⁴⁶ And having-said-farewell to-them, he-went-away into the mountain to-pray.

⁴⁷ And evening having-come, the boat was in the middle of-the sea, and he alone upon the land.

⁴⁸ And having-seen them straining in the rowing — for the wind was against them — about the fourth watch of-the night, he-comes to them, walking upon the sea. And he-was-wanting to-pass-by them.

⁴⁹ But they, having-seen him walking upon the sea, thought that it-is a-phantom, and cried-out;

⁵⁰ for all saw him and were-troubled. But immediately he-spoke with them and says to-them, "Take-courage! I AM. Do not fear."

⁵¹ And he-went-up to them into the boat, and the wind ceased. And they-were exceedingly amazed in themselves,

⁵² for they-did not understand concerning the loaves, but their heart was having-been-hardened.

⁵³ And having-crossed-over to the land, they-came to Gennesaret and anchored.

⁵⁴ And having-come-out of-them from the boat, immediately having-recognized him,

⁵⁵ they-ran-about that whole region and began to-carry-about upon the pallets the-ones having badly, to-wherever they-were-hearing that he-is.

⁵⁶ And wherever he-was-entering — into villages or into cities or into countryside — in the marketplaces they-were-placing the-ones-being-sick and were-imploring him that they-might-touch even the fringe of-his garment. And as-many-as touched him were-being-saved.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **6:3 — "the carpenter" (Ο ΤΕΚΤΩΝ):** Sinaiticus reads "the carpenter" — Jesus himself is the τέκτων (woodworker/builder). Some manuscripts read "the carpenter's son" (cf. Matthew 13:55). Sinaiticus's reading is likely original; scribes may have changed it to avoid identifying Jesus directly with manual labor.

2. **6:14 — "they were saying":** Sinaiticus has the plural "they were saying" — public opinion. Some manuscripts read "he [Herod] was saying."

3. **6:22 — "his daughter Herodias":** Sinaiticus reads "his daughter Herodias" (ΑΥΤΟΥ ΗΡΩΔΙΑΔΟΣ). This likely means "the daughter of Herodias herself" rather than that Herod's own daughter was named Herodias. According to Josephus, the daughter's name was Salome.

4. **6:48 — "he was wanting to pass by them" (ΗΘΕΛΕΝ ΠΑΡΕΛΘΕΙΝ ΑΥΤΟΥΣ):** This enigmatic detail — Jesus wanted to "pass by" the disciples — likely echoes OT theophanies where God "passes by" (Exodus 33:19-22; 1 Kings 19:11). It is a revelatory act, not an intention to ignore them.

5. **6:50 — "I AM" (ΕΓΩ ΕΙΜΙ):** While this can simply mean "it is I," the phrase echoes the divine self-revelation of Exodus 3:14 (LXX: ΕΓΩ ΕΙΜΙ Ο ΩΝ). In context — walking on the sea, the theophanic "passing by" — the divine overtones are unmistakable.

6. **6:52 — "their heart was hardened":** Mark's devastating editorial comment. The disciples' failure to understand is connected to their hearts being hardened (πεπωρωμένη) — the same word used of Pharaoh in the Exodus.

— EMET, AI Translator
